summary: Matched several small fighter functions across common Attack100/item-scope/catch collision handlers, Kirby Zelda-copy collision, and Yoshi shield/guard logic, while also touching Popo Side-B formatting/include order. The main value was replacing placeholder stubs with simple callback-wrapper implementations and adding needed header prototypes for shared helper callbacks.
what_changed: Implemented ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Coll as a small wrapper calling ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_800D7CEC).
Implemented ftCo_ItemScopeFire_Coll, ftCo_Catch_Coll, ftCo_CatchDash_Coll, and ftCo_CatchWait_Coll as collision wrapper functions using ft_800841B8 with the appropriate callback helpers.
Added prototypes in ftCo_Attack100.h for fn_800D7CEC, fn_800D82EC, fn_800D8E30, and fn_800DA440 so the newly implemented wrappers can refer to their callbacks cleanly.

Implemented ftKb_ZdSpecialAirN_Coll with the same air/ground collision transition pattern as nearby Kirby special collision code: if ft_80081D0C(gobj) is not GA_Ground, call ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105F3C(gobj).

Implemented ftYs_Shield_8012C600, including motion-state setup, hitlag/post-hitlag callbacks, shield part visibility handling, inlineA0 call, guard velocity calculation from common data and lightshield state, and final guard helper calls.

Implemented ftYs_GuardOn_1_Anim, including ftCo_80093BC0, updating fp->mv.ys.guard.x0, ftCo_80092BCC, effect spawning through spawnEffect, guard transition through ftCo_800928CC, and ftYs_Init_8012B8A4 while frames remain.
Reformatted and reordered includes/long lines in ftPp_SpecialS.c; the diff excerpt mostly shows style-preserving line wrapping rather than substantive Popo behavior changes.
smart_moves: Recognized multiple previously stubbed common collision functions as thin wrappers around existing transition helpers plus callback functions, minimizing risk and avoiding unnecessary reconstruction.
Kept callback helper functions named by address where no semantic name was evident, while exposing prototypes with address comments in the header for consistency with the surrounding file.
Used existing project idioms for air/ground collision checks in Kirby: compare ft_80081D0C(gobj) against GA_Ground and dispatch to the appropriate transition helper.
For Yoshi shield matching, preserved unexplained stack-padding declarations such as u8 _[16] and u8 _[12], indicating attention to exact codegen/layout requirements.
Reused nearby guard/common helpers instead of open-coding behavior: ftCo_80092450, ftCo_80091D58, ftCo_80093BC0, ftCo_80092BCC, ftCo_800925A4, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, and ftCo_800928CC.
decomp_lessons: Many fighter Coll functions are one-call wrappers that select a generic ground/air collision transition helper and pass a callback; search nearby action-state collision functions before attempting a complex reconstruction.
When adding a matched wrapper that references an internal callback, update the corresponding header with the callback prototype and address comment to keep callsites type-safe and searchable.
Yoshi shield code depends on shared common-data constants and character init constants; preserving exact float expressions and branch structure can matter for matching.
Unexplained local padding arrays in decomp output may be intentional for stack layout and should not be removed just because they have no semantic behavior.
Formatting-only churn in large fighter files can obscure the meaningful changes; future reviewers should separate mechanical line wrapping/include ordering from actual matching additions.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action functions use the ftCo_ prefix with action and callback suffixes such as ItemScopeAirRapid_Coll, ItemScopeFire_Coll, Catch_Coll, CatchDash_Coll, and CatchWait_Coll.
Unidentified helper callbacks remain address-based with the fn_800Dxxxx form, such as fn_800D7CEC, fn_800D82EC, fn_800D8E30, and fn_800DA440.
Kirby character-specific functions use ftKb_ and encode copied-special context in names such as ftKb_ZdSpecialAirN_Coll.
Yoshi shield/guard functions use ftYs_Shield_8012xxxx for unresolved shield helpers and ftYs_GuardOn_1_Anim for action-state callbacks.
Motion-state callback suffixes retain project-standard roles: _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implement placeholder `/// #function` stubs as minimal wrapper bodies when the target assembly is just a call to a known transition helper with a function-pointer callback.

Use the correct generic collision helper based on action context: ft_800841B8 for several grounded/common collision wrappers and ft_80082C74 for the air rapid scope collision wrapper shown in the diff.
Preserve explicit casts for callback assignment in Yoshi shield code, e.g. assigning ftCo_80093240 and ftCo_800932DC to Fighter hitlag callback fields.
Preserve stack-shaping dummy locals when matching functions with sensitive stack layout.
Keep float calculations in the apparent original expression order for shield velocity/lightshield math rather than simplifying algebraically.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided slice. The context reports one issue comment but the human text excerpt is empty, so no concrete reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftCo_*_Coll stubs that are likely thin wrappers around ft_800841B8 or ft_80082C74 with an address-named callback.
Audit ftCo_Attack100.h for missing prototypes of address-named callback helpers used by newly matched functions.
Compare nearby Yoshi guard functions for opportunities to replace ftYs_Shield_8012xxxx address names with semantic names once behavior is understood.
Separate formatting-only diffs in ftPp_SpecialS.c from real matching changes when mining this PR for Popo behavior.
